domain_description: CEO-facing requirements-gathering design -- the COO peels feature scope from the outside in, freezes the approved human-facing onion, and only then derives the technical requirement package.
focus_areas: Outside-in scope shaping: topic, goal, expected result, and success view are clarified before deep detail (RQG-001, RQG-003)
Question discipline: one business-level clarification question at a time (RQG-002)
Feature structure: major parts are defined before per-part clarification (RQG-004)
UI alignment: mockup or preview loop is used when UI meaning matters (RQG-005)
Working truth: scope is captured in an artifact, not left only in chat (RQG-006)
Boundary clarity: constraints, non-goals, and open business decisions are explicit before freeze (RQG-007)
Freeze gate: the whole onion is shown back to the CEO for explicit approval (RQG-008)
Meaning preservation: the technical requirement package preserves the approved human scope without inventing business decisions (RQG-009)
Readiness handoff: the package is self-checked against the rulebook before CTO handoff (RQG-010)

severity_definitions.blocking: Prevents safe scope freeze or creates a high risk that later phases will guess core product meaning. Must be fixed before freeze.
severity_definitions.major: Significant scope-shaping gap that will cause downstream pushback, redesign, or misaligned implementation. Must be fixed before freeze.
severity_definitions.minor: Small clarity or framing issue. Can be frozen with conditions if the business meaning is still intact.
severity_definitions.suggestion: Nice-to-have improvement. Does not affect freeze decision.

full_sweep_rounds: first and last (when no pushbacks found)
